title: Checking Tefillin and Mezuzot: When and How Often
title_zh: 检查经匣和门框经文:何时检查及多久一次
title_he: בדיקת תפילין ומזוזות: מתי וכמה פעמים
description: The requirement to have the parchments inside your tefillin and mezuzot checked by a sofer (scribe), how often, and what can go wrong.

chinese_historical_periods_mentioned.context: Referenced when discussing the giving of the Torah at Sinai (~3,300 years ago)
Referenced when discussing Rabbi Judah the Prince and the compilation of the Mishnah
Referenced when discussing the Babylonian Talmud tractate Menachot
Referenced when discussing the Shulchan Aruch

practical_guidance: Check tefillin at least twice every seven years
Check mezuzot at least every 3.5 years
More frequent checking recommended in humid climates (Asia)
Check immediately after water exposure, drops, or structural damage
Only qualified sofrim can perform proper checking
Keep a checking log with dates and results
Cost: $20-40 per mezuzah, $50-100 per tefillin set
Never assume used items are kosher - always have them checked
Resources in Asia: Chabad Houses in major cities, mailing to Israel/Hong Kong/Bangkok/Singapore
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
